texto: I just like the idea of looking back at my life and feeling like I made different choices than everyone else, you know? Most people are inherently boring if you really dig deep. They don't want much, they don't veer from their chosen path, and they're generally scared of change. I don't know, at least that's how my grandfather tells it. I don't want to be like fifty and realize that I was one of those people who didn't bother to think outside the box.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Margaux Froley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I just like the idea of looking back at my life and feeling like I made different choices than ev…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, change, change-your-life pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e mudanca.

They don't want much, they don't veer from their chosen path, and they're generally scared of change. I don't know, at least that's how my grandfather tells it. I don't want to be like fifty and realize that I was one of those people who didn't bother to think outside the box.

texto: Because I figure there's two kinds of people in the world. The ones who do everything that's laid out for them, the supposed-tos, and then there's the people that look above it and do what they want to do. I prefer the latter, but maybe that's just me. A not-supposed to.
